title: Generate k6 tests with Grafana Assistant

blocks.content:
Welcome to the learning path for **developers**, **QA engineers**, and **SREs** who want to turn plain-language goals into runnable **Grafana Cloud k6** scripts without starting from a blank file. Most people complete the milestones in about **30–40 minutes**.

**k6 Script Authoring** is a **mode of Grafana Assistant** focused on performance tests: you describe or attach context, and Assistant returns structured JavaScript with checks, thresholds, and URL grouping so metrics stay readable in Grafana.

This path is a **hands-on companion** to [Use k6 Script Authoring mode](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/author-run/k6-script-authoring-mode/): milestones sequence the work; the doc remains the source for limitations, prompts, and UI detail. It sits **alongside** manual-script paths such as [Run your first k6 performance test](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/run-first-k6-test/) and [Establish a performance baseline with k6](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/establish-k6-baseline/)—those teach how k6 behaves; this path teaches how **Assistant accelerates** authoring once you know the vocabulary.

**Not the right moment?** Skip this path if **Grafana Assistant** or **Performance** testing is not enabled for your stack or role—use the manual k6 paths until your admin turns Assistant on.

**Trust but verify:** Assistant output is a **starting point**. Always review generated scripts for correctness, secrets, destructive endpoints, and realistic load before you run against production or shared environments.

blocks.alt: Grafana Assistant in k6 Script Authoring mode with Follow-up choices to write a new test, discover and generate from telemetry, or work with an existing script

blocks.content:
## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Explain how **k6 Script Authoring** differs from general Assistant use and when to open it
- Generate a new k6 script from a prompt, including smoke, load, stress, spike, or soak style goals
- Use metrics, logs, and traces plus optional OpenAPI or GitHub context to discover realistic endpoints
- Analyze and improve an existing k6 script for cardinality, checks, thresholds, and lifecycle issues
- Convert Postman collections or curl commands into k6
- Move a generated script into Grafana Cloud k6 and interpret a first run

When you finish, you should have **opened k6 Script Authoring**, walked at least one **Follow-up** workflow end to end, and have a script you can **paste into Grafana Cloud k6** for a conservative first run.

## Before you begin

Before you use k6 Script Authoring, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- **Grafana Assistant** enabled on your stack. If you do not see the **Assistant** icon, ask your administrator to enable it. If Assistant or **Testing & synthetics** > **Performance** is unavailable on your plan or region, pause this path and use the manual k6 learning paths until access is available.
- Familiarity with k6 basics: what a **virtual user (VU)** is, how **stages** or duration shape load, and what **checks** and **thresholds** do. If you are new to k6, complete [Run your first k6 performance test](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/run-first-k6-test/) first—you do not need to write scripts from memory, but you need to recognize those ideas in generated code.
- Optional: metrics, logs, or traces in Grafana Cloud for the **Discover & generate** workflow, and an OpenAPI spec or GitHub repository URL if you want richer requests.

## Terms used in this path

- **Virtual user (VU)**: a simulated concurrent user loop running your script logic; VU count controls parallelism.
- **Check**: a pass or fail assertion on a response (for example status code or JSON field). Refer to [Checks](https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/using-k6/checks/).
- **Threshold**: a pass or fail gate on a metric (for example `p(95) < 500ms`). Refer to [Thresholds](https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/using-k6/thresholds/).
- **Metric cardinality**: each distinct combination of metric **labels** becomes its own time series in Grafana. Raw URLs in labels can create **high cardinality** (thousands of series) and slow queries—**URL grouping** collapses dynamic paths into stable names. Refer to [Tags and groups](https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/using-k6/tags-and-groups/).
- **RPS**: requests per second observed or targeted.
- **p95 latency**: the 95th percentile response time—95% of requests complete faster than this value.
- **Infinity** data source: a Grafana data source that can fetch JSON or YAML from URLs (including some private URLs)—used in the product doc to load an OpenAPI spec when a URL is not public. Refer to [Infinity data source plugin](https://grafana.com/docs/plugins/yesoreyeram-infinity-datasource/latest/).
- **Tempo** and **Pyroscope**: optional trace and profile backends; Assistant may add hooks so k6 results line up with traces or profiles in Grafana Cloud.

## Troubleshooting

Start with [Use k6 Script Authoring mode](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/author-run/k6-script-authoring-mode/) for access steps, example prompts, and **known limitations**.

If your issue is:

- **No Assistant icon or mode**: confirm Assistant is enabled for your stack and your role; contact your Grafana admin. The product doc lists access requirements.
- **Discover & generate** finds no endpoints or the wrong service: confirm the **service name** matches telemetry, that metrics, logs, or traces exist in Grafana Cloud for that service, and that your user can query those signals.
- **GitHub or OpenAPI** context fails: check repository visibility, tokens or MCP setup your org requires, and any rate or size limits—details stay in the canonical doc.
- **Script errors in the k6 editor** (validation or save failures): compare options against [k6 options](https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/using-k6/options/) and Grafana Cloud k6 constraints; paste the error back into Assistant and ask for a fix.
- **Run fails** (timeouts, DNS, auth): capture the failure output, verify target URLs and secrets, reduce VUs for a smoke run, then iterate with Assistant using the error text.

For Grafana Cloud k6 run issues beyond Assistant, refer to [Grafana Cloud k6](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/) and linked troubleshooting topics there.

## More to explore

Where it makes sense, milestones point you to related docs and paths so you can go deeper without leaving the main storyline.
